perturbation.original_text: Each party acknowledges the sensitive and secret nature of the Confidential Information it will have access to d uring the term of the Project and agrees that it will treat such Confidential Information as strictly confidential and shall exercise the same degree of care in the protection of the Confidential Information as the receiving party exercises with respect to its own proprietary property and trade secrets, but in no event shall it be less than a reasonable degree of care given the nature of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party acknowledges the sensitive and secret nature of the Confidential Material it will have access to d uring the term of the Project and agrees that it will treat such Confidential Information as strictly confidential and shall exercise the same degree of care in the protection of the Confidential Information as the receiving party exercises with respect to its own proprietary property and trade secrets, but in no event shall it be less than a reasonable degree of care given the nature of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Confidential Material' in the first instance creates uncertainty. The agreement defines 'Confidential Information'. Is 'Confidential Material' something different? If so, is it subject to the same protections? In the rest of the sentence, it continues referencing 'Confidential Information', due to this, it makes it unclear which of the two should be followed. If the obligations and protection only applies to Confidential Information then Material is not being protected, weakening the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party acknowledges the sensitive and secret nature of the Confidential Information it will have access to d uring the term of the Project and agrees that it will treat such Confidential Information as strictly confidential and shall exercise the same degree of care in the protection of the Confidential Information as the receiving party exercises with respect to its own proprietary property and trade secrets, but in no event shall it be less than a reasonable degree of care given the nature of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Confidential Material' in the first instance. The agreement defines 'Confidential Information', and the contradicted text also refers to 'Confidential Information'. By introducing 'Confidential Material' and continuing to reference 'Confidential Information' in the same sentence, it creates ambiguity and uncertainty about which term the obligations and protections apply to. If the protections only apply to 'Confidential Information', then 'Confidential Material' is not being protected, which is a contradiction of the contradicted text, making the original text contradictory.

perturbation.original_text: Each party acknowledges that the other, because of the nature of the Confidential Information, would suffer irreparable harm in the event of a material breach of the provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement in that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate for such a breach, and that in the event of any material breach or threatened mat erial breach by a party receiving Confidential Information from the other party of any such provisions, a party shall be entitled, in addition to such other legal or equitable remedies which might be available, to injunctive relief in any court of competen t jurisdiction against the threatened material breach or continuation of any such material breach without showing or proving any actual damages sustained by it. Moreover, any such award of relief to the discloser of such Confidential Information shall inc lude recovery of all actual and reasonable costs associated with enforcement of this Agreement including, without limitation, attorneys’ fees.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party acknowledges that the other, because of the nature of the Protected Data, would suffer irreparable harm in the event of a material breach of the provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement in that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate for such a breach, and that in the event of any material breach or threatened mat erial breach by a party receiving Confidential Information from the other party of any such provisions, a party shall be entitled, in addition to such other legal or equitable remedies which might be available, to injunctive relief in any court of competen t jurisdiction against the threatened material breach or continuation of any such material breach without showing or proving any actual damages sustained by it. Moreover, any such award of relief to the discloser of such Confidential Information shall inc lude recovery of all actual and reasonable costs associated with enforcement of this Agreement including, without limitation, attorneys’ fees.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Data' creates confusion about the scope of protection. If 'Protected Data' isn't defined, it's unclear if it aligns with the definition of 'Confidential Information'. This creates a direct contradiction later in the same paragraph, since the text goes back to referencing Confidential Information and not Protected Data, which weakens the enforceability of the injunctive relief, since a party could state the material breached wasn't Confidential Information, it was Protected Data. Since Protected Data is never properly defined, this creates confusion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party acknowledges that the other, because of the nature of the Confidential Information, would suffer irreparable harm in the event of a material breach of the provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement in that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate for such a breach, and that in the event of any material breach or threatened mat erial breach by a party receiving Confidential Information from the other party of any such provisions, a party shall be entitled, in addition to such other legal or equitable remedies which might be available, to injunctive relief in any court of competen t jurisdiction against the threatened material breach or continuation of any such material breach without showing or proving any actual damages sustained by it. Moreover, any such award of relief to the discloser of such Confidential Information shall inc lude recovery of all actual and reasonable costs associated with enforcement of this Agreement including, without limitation, attorneys’ fees.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Data' in the first instance within the paragraph. The contradicted text, which is actually the original text, refers consistently to 'Confidential Information'. The 'Perturbation Explanation' states that this creates confusion because 'Protected Data' is never defined, leading to uncertainty about whether it aligns with the definition of 'Confidential Information', thereby weakening the enforceability of injunctive relief since a party could claim that the material breached was 'Protected Data' but not 'Confidential Information'. This misalignment of terminology creates a direct conflict within the same paragraph because after the first instance of 'Protected Data' is used, the text reverts back to using 'Confidential Information'. This creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The representations and obligations of each party relative to the Confidential Information shall survive the expiration or termination of this Confidentiality Agreement for a period of the longer of three (3) years or for such time as such Confidential Information remains a protectable trade secret under applicable law.
perturbation.changed_text: The representations and obligations of each party relative to the Private Data shall survive the expiration or termination of this Confidentiality Agreement for a period of the longer of three (3) years or for such time as such Confidential Information remains a protectable trade secret under applicable law.
perturbation.explanation: The term Confidential Information is being replaced with Private Data. This creates ambiguity about whether 'Private Data' falls under the definition of Confidential Information. Also, the obligations may no longer apply to Private Data, potentially shortening the duration of protection. This is creating an in-text contradiction, in which the Confidential Information protections are still in place, while Private Data, a term that is being used in replace of the original, doesn't get the same protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The representations and obligations of each party relative to the Confidential Information shall survive the expiration or termination of this Confidentiality Agreement for a period of the longer of three (3) years or for such time as such Confidential Information remains a protectable trade secret under applicable law.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text are the same, both referring to 'Confidential Information'. However, the changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Private Data' in the first instance. The contradiction arises because while obligations initially applied to 'Confidential Information,' the modification implies that the same protections may not automatically extend to 'Private Data.' This misaligned terminology creates uncertainty and a potential weakening of data protection within the contract, satisfying the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition for in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Neither party makes or intends to make any warranty or representation of any kind concerning the Confidential Information, except each party represents and warrants that it has the right to disclose Confidential Information to Receiving Party. Confidentia l Information is provided only for discussion purposes, is provided on an “As Is” basis, and Receiving Party relies on Confidential Information at its own risk.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither party makes or intends to make any warranty or representation of any kind concerning the Confidential Details, except each party represents and warrants that it has the right to disclose Confidential Information to Receiving Party. Confidentia l Information is provided only for discussion purposes, is provided on an “As Is” basis, and Receiving Party relies on Confidential Information at its own risk.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Details' in the first instance raises the question: Are they the same? The agreement never defines 'Confidential Details'. If they are different, the disclaimer of warranty might not apply to 'Confidential Information' as defined earlier in the agreement. This is creating a contradiction where in the first sentence the parties make no warranty about the Details, but later confirm and warranty that they do have the right to disclose the Information. Since the two may not be the same, this creates an in-text contradiction, weakening the enforceability of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither party makes or intends to make any warranty or representation of any kind concerning the Confidential Information, except each party represents and warrants that it has the right to disclose Confidential Information to Receiving Party. Confidentia l Information is provided only for discussion purposes, is provided on an “As Is” basis, and Receiving Party relies on Confidential Information at its own risk.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Details' in the Changed Text, while the Contradicted Text only refers to 'Confidential Information', introduces ambiguity. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, as it potentially uses different terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. The agreement never defines 'Confidential Details', raising uncertainty as to whether it refers to the same thing as 'Confidential Information.' This casts doubt on the warranty disclaimer and the disclosure rights, leading to an in-text contradiction, because now there is confusion as to which the warranty applies to, creating a conflict and weakening enforceability.
